title: quekettia pygmaea
intro: Quekettia pygmaea, also known as the tiny orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and promoting relaxation, as well as aiding in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
